q.en: According to the book, what is the oldest record of sea travel?
q.zh: 根据本书，最古老的航海记录是什么？

options.en: Carvings from Egypt more than 6,000 years ago
options.zh: 六千多年前埃及的石刻记录

options.en: Greek paintings according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 希腊的画作（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the oldest record of sea travel comes from Egypt, where people carved images of their journeys over 6,000 years ago.
explain.zh: 书中说最古老的航海记录来自埃及，人们在六千多年前就把航行刻在了石头上。

q.en: What early boat did many cultures make by hollowing out a tree trunk?
q.zh: 许多文明都把树干挖空来制造哪种早期船只？

explain.en: Both Egyptians and Native Americans in the Americas built dugout canoes by hollowing out tree trunks.
explain.zh: 埃及人和美洲原住民都通过挖空树干来制造独木舟。

q.en: The Polynesians built an early version of which modern boat to sail the open ocean?
q.zh: 波利尼西亚人造出了哪种现代船只的早期版本，用以航行于开阔的海洋？

options.en: A trireme according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 三层桨战船（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A catamaran (two canoes joined by a deck)
options.zh: 双体船（两艘独木舟用甲板相连）

explain.en: Polynesian sailors joined two canoes with poles and a deck, an early version of the modern catamaran.
explain.zh: 波利尼西亚水手用木杆和甲板把两艘独木舟相连，那是现代双体船的早期版本。

q.en: What does 'hull' mean in the book?

options.en: The main outer body or shell of a boat
A steering pole according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一根舵杆（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'hull' as the main body of a sailing vessel.
explain.zh: 词汇表将'hull'定义为帆船的主体（船身）。

q.en: What were Viking longships able to do that made them hard to defeat?
q.zh: 维京长船具备什么能力，使它难以被击败？

options.en: Travel fast (up to 15 knots) and easily change direction
options.zh: 航速快（可达15节）且易于转向

options.en: Carry 1,000 warriors according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 能载1000名战士（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Longships looked the same front and back, so they could change direction easily, and reached speeds up to 15 knots.
explain.zh: 长船首尾同形，便于掉头，航速更可达15节。

q.en: On Magellan's voyage around the world, how many ships and crew made it back?
q.zh: 在麦哲伦的环球航行中，有多少艘船和多少名船员成功归来？

options.en: Five ships and 260 crew according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 五艘船和260名船员（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Of five ships and 260 sailors, only one ship and eighteen crew completed the voyage; Magellan was killed in the Philippines.
explain.zh: 五艘船、260名水手中，仅一艘船和十八人完成航行；麦哲伦本人在菲律宾被杀。

q.en: How did Chinese explorer Zheng He's ships compare to European ships?
q.zh: 中国探险家郑和的船与欧洲船只相比如何？

options.en: They were exactly the same size according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它们大小完全一样（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: His junks were much larger - the biggest could hold all three of Columbus's ships on its deck
options.zh: 他的宝船大得多——最大的能容下哥伦布的三艘船同置于甲板

explain.en: Zheng He's largest junk was 121 meters long and could have held all three of Columbus's ships on its deck.
explain.zh: 郑和最大的宝船长121米，甲板上足以容下哥伦布的三艘船。

q.en: Why does the author mention that the Chinese emperor later banned ocean voyages and destroyed the treasure ships?
q.zh: 作者为什么要提到中国皇帝后来禁止远洋航行并毁掉了宝船？

options.en: To explain a fishing law according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了解释一条渔业法规（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To show how history might have been different if China had kept exploring
options.zh: 为了说明若中国持续探索，历史可能会大不相同

explain.en: The author notes that if China had continued, Americans might speak Chinese - highlighting a turning point in history.
explain.zh: 作者指出，若中国继续探索，美洲人或许会说中文——借此凸显历史的转折点。

q.en: How did Captain James Cook keep his crew healthy on long voyages?
q.zh: 詹姆斯·库克船长在长途航行中如何保持船员健康？

options.en: By letting them sleep in beds according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 让他们睡在床上（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: By having them eat limes and cabbages (vitamin C) to avoid scurvy
options.zh: 让他们吃酸橙和卷心菜（维生素C）以预防坏血病

explain.en: Cook fed his crew limes and cabbages so they got vitamin C and avoided scurvy, which had killed many sailors before.
explain.zh: 库克让船员吃酸橙和卷心菜以摄取维生素C，从而免于坏血病——此前许多水手因此丧生。

q.en: According to the book's conclusion, what largely replaced wind power by the 1870s?
q.zh: 根据本书的结尾，到了19世纪70年代，是什么大体取代了风力？

explain.en: By the 1870s the steam engine largely replaced wind power, launching the industrial revolution in shipbuilding.
explain.zh: 到了19世纪70年代，蒸汽机大体取代了风力，掀起了造船业的工业革命。

prompt.en: The book shows how ships changed from dugout canoes to steam-powered vessels. If you could design one ship for exploration today, what would it look like and what would it be used to discover?
prompt.zh: 本书展示了船只如何从独木舟演变为蒸汽动力船。如果你今天能设计一艘用于探索的船，它会是什么样子，又用来探索什么？

en: Think about what environment it would explore (ocean, sky, or space).
Include one feature that solves a problem older ships had, such as speed, safety, or cargo.
zh: 想想它将探索什么环境（海洋、天空或太空）。
加入一项能解决旧船某一问题（如速度、安全或载货）的设计。
